name: 1787 Boqueron earthquake
text: Estimated M~8 off northern Puerto Rico — the largest event in the island's written history. Damaged churches across the island.

name: 1867 Virgin Islands earthquake & tsunami
text: M~7.3 in the Anegada Passage. Generated a tsunami that struck the Virgin Islands and eastern Puerto Rico.

name: 1918 Mona Passage earthquake & tsunami
text: M7.1 west of Puerto Rico. A tsunami reached the west coast within minutes; about 116 people died, many from the tsunami. The defining event for PR tsunami preparedness.

name: 2020 Guanica sequence (M6.4)
text: A prolonged sequence in southwestern PR. The Jan 7, 2020 M6.4 was the most damaging PR earthquake in over a century — collapsed buildings, island-wide power loss, thousands displaced. Thousands of aftershocks.
